description_long: This is a large, freestanding outdoor stone oven constructed from irregularly shaped, weathered gray and brown stones. The oven features a prominent arched opening at the front, which serves as the main cooking chamber, and a smaller arched compartment below, likely for firewood storage or ash collection. The structure is robust and monolithic, with a tall, rectangular chimney extending upward from the main body to vent smoke. The stonework is rough and uneven, giving the oven a rustic, hand-built appearance typical of traditional masonry ovens found in parks or communal urban spaces. The sides and back are solid stone with no visible openings or decorative elements, emphasizing durability and function. The oven's surface is mottled with patches of discoloration, suggesting exposure to the elements and frequent use.
description: A large, rustic stone oven with a tall chimney, arched front openings, and a weathered, hand-built appearance. The structure is robust and designed for outdoor use, with rough, uneven stonework.
description_view_0: Front view showing two arched openings, the main oven chamber above and a storage or ash compartment below, with the tall chimney rising above.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid, rough stonework and the full height of the chimney, with no visible openings or features.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a flat, uninterrupted stone surface and the back of the chimney, with no access points or decorative details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the solid stone construction and a possible small metal handle or fixture on the side.
attribute_control_panel_design: No control panel is visible; the oven appears to be manually operated with no electronic or mechanical controls present.
attribute_door_handle_type: A small, simple metal handle is visible on the side, likely protruding and mounted vertically, with a utilitarian design and no decorative features.
